title: derris macrocarpa
intro: Derris macrocarpa, also known as the Makopa or Makopa vine, is a climbing plant native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and has also been used as a natural remedy for digestive issues and wounds. Derris macrocarpa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its health benefits.
